records.ai_summary: Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank loan to fund Atkins' UK office in Bristol.
records.notes: Loan to fund the regional UK office of global engineering firm Atkins, located at Aztec West Business Park in Bristol

records.ai_summary: Lloyds Bank Commercial Banking lends to Halton Housing to fund 1,000 new homes.
records.notes: Loan to fund 1,000 new homes across the county

records.ai_summary: Lotus Capital Partners lends to Wainbridge for the conversion of townhouses into luxury apartments.
records.notes: Loan for the conversion of a row of townhouses into 12 ultra-luxury apartments

records.ai_summary: OakNorth provided a 5-year loan to Ankor Property Group with a 65% LTV ratio.
records.notes: 5-year loan, priced at 4.5% plus Libor. The debt facility’s LTV ratio is 65%

records.ai_summary: Barings Real Estate provided a 7-year fixed-rate loan to Alrov Group subsidiary to refinance existing debt for a 159-room hotel.
records.notes: 7-year, fixed-rate, first-mortgage loan is secured against the 159-room hotel. The loan, which represents a 47% LTV, will be used to refinance existing debt

records.ai_summary: Institutional investors lent to Great Portland Estates following a successful tender offer.
records.notes: Followed GPE's successful tender offer for a legacy debenture

records.ai_summary: Ingenious Real Estate lends to Ensco Homes for the development of 50 residential units.
records.notes: Loan will fund the development of 50 residential units

records.ai_summary: Ingenious Real Estate provides loan to finance offices and flats.
records.notes: Loan will finance 24,000 sq ft of offices and 39 flats

records.ai_summary: Al Rayan Bank issues debut Islamic UK RMBS with a profit rate of three-month sterling Libor plus 0.80%.
records.notes: Debut Islamic UK RMBS in a non-Muslim country, providing a profit rate of three-month sterling Libor plus 0.80% for its only senior tranche

records.ai_summary: CBRE-managed Evergreen 2 fund provides loan to Manchester Science Partnerships for development project in England's North-West.
records.notes: First loan from the second Evergreen fund, an EU vehicle designed to support development projects in England’s North-West

records.ai_summary: LaSalle Investment Management provided a 5-year mezzanine facility to Lone Star to refinance a portfolio of 40 hotels.
records.notes: 5-year mezzanine facility to refinance a portfolio of 40 hotels managed by the Shearings Leisure Group

records.ai_summary: Metro Bank lends to Z Hotels to acquire and convert an office building into a hotel near Trafalgar Square.
records.notes: Loan will be used to acquire a 5-storey office building just off Trafalgar Square to be converted into a hotel

records.ai_summary: A private investor has secured a £28.5m development loan from Titlestone and a £14m loan from Shawbrook.
records.notes: £28.5m, 2-year, 60% LTV development loan provided by Titlestone and £14m, 70% LTV loan from Shawbrook with same maturity

records.ai_summary: Investec provides senior facility to Meadow Partners for site acquisition and planning to convert mixed-use buildings in Docklands into residential scheme.
records.notes: Senior facility to finance the site acquisition and planning process to convert 6 mixed-use office and retail buildings, situated in Docklands business district, into a residential scheme

records.ai_summary: BNP Paribas provided a loan to London & Regional for a hotel refurbishment project.
records.notes: Loan with a maturity of between 5 and 7 years. London & Regional submitted a planning application 2 years ago for an extensive refurbishment of the hotel, to include new luxury flats

records.ai_summary: TH Real Estate lends to Samsung SRA Investment Management to fund the acquisition of a 430,000 sq ft office building.
records.notes: Loan to fund the acquistion of the 430,000 sq ft office building, which has been comprehensively refurbished and is fully let to a range of tenants

records.ai_summary: NewRiver issues a 10-year senior unsecured bond with a 3.5% fixed coupon to repay existing facilities and for general corporate purposes.
records.notes: Senior, unsecured bond with a term of 10 years and a fixed coupon of 3.5%. Proceeds will be used to repay existing secured facilities totalling £177m, existing unsecured facilities totalling £50m and for general corporate purposes

records.ai_summary: OakNorth provides development loan to Carlton James for construction of 13 flats.
records.notes: Development loan to fund the construction of 13 flats with a targeted exit date of December 2019

records.ai_summary: Fortwell Capital renewed a revolving development finance facility for LNT Care Developments for a further 4.5 years.
records.notes: Renewed revolving development finance facility for a further 4.5 years

records.ai_summary: LBS Properties is developing 83 apartments at 211-227 Hackney Road.
records.notes: The new development will occupy a 0.56 acre site at 211-227 Hackney Road, replacing a disused bingo hall with 83 apartments

records.ai_summary: Beni Stabili issued 10-year notes with a 2.375% fixed annual coupon.
records.notes: Notes with 10-year maturity and a 2.375% fixed annual coupon. The bonds were priced at a spread of 133bps over the reference mid swap rate

records.ai_summary: Barings Real Estate provided a 10-year fixed rate loan to Schroder Real Estate Investment Management.
records.notes: The 40% LTV, 10-year fixed rate loan is secured against the recently completed 310,000 sq ft office building

records.ai_summary: Lloyds Bank provided a loan to Romulus for a portfolio of office and retail assets in Hammersmith and Fulham.
records.notes: Loan provided at a margin discount, in line with Lloyds’ £1 billion ‘green’ commercial property lending initiative. The portfolio comprises several office and retail assets in Hammersmith and Fulham town centres

records.ai_summary: LendInvest provides development finance loan to Yogo Group for residential project.
records.notes: Development finance loan to fund the part-conversion and rebuild of the former Thomas Lipton Care Home, as well as the construction of new residential units

records.ai_summary: Fortwell Capital grants a 9 month extension to a 2 year construction facility to Prime City Developments with an LTC of up to 90%.
records.notes: 9 month extension to a 2 year construction facility and LTC of up to 90%
2-year loan with and a LTC ratio of 80%
Loan to fund the development comprising 200,000 sq ft of retail and leisure accommodation in the centre of Rochdale
2-year development loan for the first phase of the home regeneration scheme ‘The Ropeworks’
Loan to finance a PRS project in early-construction phase, which is expected to be completed by March 2019
5-year development facility, priced at 3.5% and with a LTC ratio of 45%
The finance is due for repayment in December 2024 and carries an initial interest rate of 3.28%, with interest on £31m varying with movements in 3-month Libor

records.lender: NatWest, Lloyds Bank Commercial Real Estate, Greater Manchester Housing Fund
records.notes: Development debt facility with a LTV ratio of 44 percent. The build-to-rent project is scheduled for completion in Q1 2021
3-year whole loan providing development finance for a office building at the Embassy Gardens regeneration project
3-year funding package provided at a margin discount, in line with Lloyds’ £1 billion ‘green’ commercial property lending initiative
Long-term £75m loan from Aviva at a fixed interest rate of 3.1% per year; and 3-year £30m revolving credit facility from Lloyds, at rates ranging from 155bps to 175bps over Libor
30-year bond issued to complete transactions from 2018
Revolving credit facility for the firm's Diversified Property Fund for Charities
Notes carry a coupon of 1.5% with an average maturity of 5.3 years

records.lender: Barclays Bank, ABN AMRO Bank, BNP Paribas, Niederlassung Deutschland, Société Générale (Frankfurt branch)
records.notes: Facility for Berlin-focused REIT, which portfolio comprised 20,224 units by end of 2017, of which 19,053 are residential units
Loan to fund a portfolio of prime office, retail and industrial properties in key locations of Paris
Pbb had already financed the property's development. The 24,000 sq m building is home to French consumer goods group L'Oréal's German headquarters

records.lender: Starwood European Real Estate Finance and lending partner
records.notes: Starwood provided a whole loan with capex facility. Duration of 5 years.
Investment facility provided to companies managed by JR AMC on behalf of a consortium of Korean investors. The tower comprises a floor space of more than 30,000 sq m

records.borrower: SPV owned by InfraRed Capital Partners
records.notes: Loan to fund the purchase of the Senie Avenue building, consisting of around 23,400 sq m office space
Loan to fund Deka's acquisition of the office building for €775m. The purchase price implies a LTV ratio of around 32%. HypoVereinsbank syndicated €100m to ING Bank
5-year mezzanine loan to finance 5 assets covering 80,000 sq m in Munich, Hannover and Dusseldorf. Provided through LREDS III fund
Starwood provided €60m of aquisition finance and an additional €9m loan to finance the conversion of 80 aparthotel units into 84 residential apartments at Westpark Apartments

records.borrower: TERRAGON AG and Tübingen-based SAX Group
records.notes: Development finance to fund 200 serviced apartments for senior citizens in Berlin. Completion is scheduled for the end of 2020
Development loan to build the shopping centre. The debt facility also provides long-term financing once the building is completed
Loan to finance a building with a total area of 26,500 sq m. Two-thirds will be used for hotel accommodation. The top 10 floors of the 32-storey building will be used as offices
Loan to fund a 30,323 sq m office portfolio, with a market value of €80m
10-year loan will refinance 270 properties which are almost fully leased

records.lender: BNP Paribas, Crédit Agricole CIB, UBI Banca
records.notes: 5-year loan for the new tower consisting of 26 floors above ground reaching over 120 metres, plus 4 underground levels below, over a total gross area of 68,000 sq m
Completion of 'NEO' is scheduled for early 2020. The building consists of a tower with 16 floors for hotel and office facilities as well as six floors of residential space

Long term loans with a maturity of 30 years. The transaction was divided in two parts: an acquisition facility of €80m provided at end of 2017 as well as a capex facility in the same amount signed in January 2018

records.lender: Natixis, SCOR, Crédit Foncier de France, Cheyne Capital
records.notes: Senior loan of €105m provided by Natixis, in a pool with SCOR and Crédit Foncier de France. The junior facility totals €27m
Portfolio consists of 12 office buildings mainly located in Hamburg, Düsseldorf, Munich, Wiesbaden and the Stuttgart region with an overall lettable space of around 89.500 sq m
Loan understood to have a LTV ratio of 65% and a term of 5 years
Each of the properties is rented to logistics operator Hermes Germany

records.borrower: Chariot Top Group (owned by Griffin Real Estate, Oaktree Capital Management, PIMCO and Redefine Properties)
records.notes: Loan backs the €1bn acquisition of the retail portfolio, which implies a 63% LTV ratio
5-year facility to refinance 12 hostels based in 8 countries across Europe. It also finances the remaining development works for the Madrid hostel due to open in 2018
Loan extends the maturity of the assets' financing by another 5 years
Loan to fund the development of 4 residential buildings within the HafenCity regeneration scheme

records.ai_summary: BlackRock enters £1bn JV with GMPF to drive primary healthcare investment.
records.title: BlackRock enters £1bn JV with GMPF to drive primary healthcare investment
records.content:
The JV has been launched with a seed portfolio of 65 primary care assets serving over 700,000 patients.
The post BlackRock enters £1bn JV with GMPF to drive primary healthcare investment appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: USS to acquire majority stake in Venn Partners to boost investment in UK housing.
records.title: USS to acquire majority stake in Venn Partners
records.content:
The deal with the UK’s largest private pension scheme will boost Venn’s capacity to invest in UK housing.
The post USS to acquire majority stake in Venn Partners appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: Forsters adds Morgan to banking and finance team from Macfarlanes.
records.title: Forsters adds Morgan to banking and finance team
records.content:
He joins from Macfarlanes, where he was as partner and had worked for over 15 years.
The post Forsters adds Morgan to banking and finance team appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: Trent & Dove secures £88m finance package to boost affordable homes.
records.title: Trent & Dove secures £88m finance package to boost affordable homes
records.content:
Scottish Widows provided £55m and Lloyds £30m to back the not-for-profit housing provider's operations in Staffordshire, Derbyshire, and  Leicestershire.
The post Trent & Dove secures £88m finance package to boost affordable homes appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: JP Morgan is seeking a business rates discount to proceed with a £3bn office project in Canary Wharf, London.
records.title: JP Morgan looks for business rates discount to unlock £3bn Canary Wharf office project
records.content:
Tower Hamlets Council is set to agree a business rates discount for the planned 3m sq ft tower, which would be the largest and most expensive office building in London.
The post JP Morgan looks for business rates discount to unlock £3bn Canary Wharf office project appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: Frogmore to exit fund management business.
records.title: Frogmore to exit fund management business
records.content:
The company confirmed the move after a series of recent administrations as it winds down its £400m FREP III value-add fund, with a host of schemes being sold off or handed back to lenders.
The post Frogmore to exit fund management business appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: Allsop pays tribute to former partner and lead auctioneer Will Clough.
records.title: Allsop pays tribute to ‘brilliant leader’ Will Clough
records.content:
He joined the company as a graduate in 2010 and later became a partner and served as lead auctioneer in Allsop’s commercial auction business.
The post Allsop pays tribute to ‘brilliant leader’ Will Clough appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: Two thirds of investors boosted their healthcare allocation in 2025.
records.title: Two thirds of investors boosted their healthcare allocation in 2025, CBRE finds
records.content:
In its 2026 Healthcare Sentiment Survey, CBRE revealed that approximately £10bn was invested in the care home sector last year, accounting for 79% of all healthcare investment.
The post Two thirds of investors boosted their healthcare allocation in 2025, CBRE finds appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: The National Housing Bank has launched with a £16bn investment plan to deliver 500,000 new homes.
records.title: Government’s £16bn National Housing Bank opens for business
records.content:
Homes England has officially launched the National Housing Bank alongside a new investment plan, which will look to deploy £16bn of debt, equity and guarantees to deliver 500,000 new homes.
The post Government’s £16bn National Housing Bank opens for business appeared first on Property Week.

records.ai_summary: Seneca sells Manchester pharmaceutical facility to occupier Seda.
records.title: Seneca sells Manchester pharmaceutical facility to occupier Seda
records.content:
The sale follows the building's repositioning as a specialist life sciences facility.
The post Seneca sells Manchester pharmaceutical facility to occupier Seda appeared first on Property Week.

insights.title: CRE Loan Volumes by Asset Class
insights.description: The total CRE loan volume for Mixed-Use/Other assets is $4,059,035,000, which is significantly higher than the total volume for Residential assets at $1,502,856,500. This suggests that investors are favoring Mixed-Use/Other assets, with a ratio of 2.7:1 compared to Residential assets. The average CRE loan size of $131,757,500 is also notable, indicating large-scale investments in the market.

insights.title: LTV Variations Across Cities
insights.description: The average LTV in London is 65.5%, which is higher than in Manchester at 40.0%. This difference in LTV may indicate varying levels of risk tolerance among investors in these cities. Furthermore, the presence of both cities in the news and deals datasets suggests that they are key markets for CRE investment, with London having a neutral news sentiment score of 0.0 and Manchester having a positive score of 1.0.

insights.title: Blind Spots in Deal Activity
insights.description: There are several cities with deal activity but no corresponding news coverage, including Edinburgh, Munich, and Leeds. This lack of visibility may pose a risk for investors, as they may not have access to timely information about market trends in these areas. The average CRE loan size in these cities is $131,757,500, which is a significant investment, and the absence of news coverage may indicate a lack of transparency in the market.

insights.title: Media Mentions and Deal Volumes
insights.description: The media mentions by asset class show a focus on Residential assets, with 3 mentions, while the deal volumes indicate a stronger presence of Mixed-Use/Other assets, with $4,059,035,000 in loans. This discrepancy may suggest that the media is not fully capturing the trends in the CRE market, and investors should look beyond media coverage to inform their decisions. The average CRE loan size of $131,757,500 also highlights the significant investment in these assets.

insights.title: Comparison to US Housing Market
insights.description: The average US residential listing price is $231,240, which is significantly lower than the average CRE loan size of $131,757,500. The ratio of 570:1 between the average CRE loan and the average US home price suggests that CRE investments are substantial and may be more resilient to market fluctuations. However, this also implies that CRE investments may be more sensitive to changes in the overall economy, given their larger scale.

insights.title: Investment in CRE vs. Residential Assets
insights.description: The total deal volume for Mixed-Use/Other assets is $4,059,035,000, which is 2.7 times the volume for Residential assets. This indicates a strong flow of capital into CRE, particularly for Mixed-Use/Other assets. The average CRE loan size of $131,757,500 also suggests that investors are committing significant funds to these investments, with a focus on Mixed-Use/Other assets. The media mentions by asset class, however, show a greater focus on Residential assets, which may indicate a potential shift in investment trends.

insights.title: News Sentiment and Deal Activity
insights.description: The news sentiment scores show a positive trend for the UK, with a score of 1.0, and Manchester, with a score of 1.0. However, London has a neutral score of 0.0, which may indicate a more stable market. The deal activity in these cities, with average LTVs of 65.5% in London and 40.0% in Manchester, suggests that investors are actively engaged in these markets, despite the varying news sentiment scores. The average CRE loan size of $131,757,500 also highlights the significant investment in these cities.

insights.title: Lack of Public Market Data
insights.description: The absence of public market data from the FMP API may indicate a lack of transparency in the CRE market, making it more challenging for investors to assess risk and make informed decisions. This, combined with the presence of blind spots in deal activity, such as Edinburgh and Munich, may pose a significant risk for investors. The average CRE loan size of $131,757,500 and the significant investment in Mixed-Use/Other assets highlight the need for more comprehensive data to mitigate these risks.
